Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Diablo Structures, Incorporated, El Cerrito Electric, Inc., And Lorentzen Co.
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Diablo Structures, Incorporated, El Cerrito Electric, Inc. and Lorentzen Co.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768. Email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

Geoff T. Sloniker, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Harold Montano and Janet Montano, who were not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Diablo Structures, Incorporated, El Cerrito Electric, Inc. or Lorentzen Co.

DEFENDANT DIABLO STRUCTURES, INCORPORTED:
Plaintiffs' complaint for personal injury was filed on June 19, 2015.
Amendment to Complaint substituting Diablo Structures, Incorporated for DOE Number 301 was filed August 13, 2015.
Plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages were personally served on defendant Diablo Structures, Incorporated's Authorized Agent for Service of Process on October 9, 2015.
Default as to defendant Diablo Structures, Incorporated was entered on April 8, 2016.
Statment of Damages was filed on April 8, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on October 21, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Harold Montano and Janet Montano, and against the Defaulted Defendant Diablo Structures, Incorporated, as follows:

Economic Damages as to plaintiff Harold Montano - $659,338.24.
Non-Economic Damages as to plaintiff Harold Montano - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Janet Montano - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $20,659,338.24.

DEFENDANT EL CERRITO ELECTRIC, INC:
Plaintiffs' complaint for personal injury was filed on June 19, 2015.
Substitute service of plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages on defendant El Cerrito Electric, Inc.'s Agent of Process occurred on July 31, 2019, and was effectuated on August 14, 2019.
Default as to defendant El Cerrito Electric, Inc. was entered on October 6, 2015.
Statment of Damages was filed on October 6, 2015.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on October 21, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Harold Montano and Janet Montano, and against the Defaulted Defendant El Cerrito Electric, Inc., as follows:

Economic Damages as to plaintiff Harold Montano - $659,338.24.
Non-Economic Damages as to plaintiff Harold Montano - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Janet Montano - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $20,659,338.24.

DEFENDANT LORENTZEN CO:
Plaintiffs' complaint for personal injury was filed on June 19, 2015.
Amendment to Complaint substituting Lorentzen Co. for DOE Number 302 was filed November 25, 2015.
Plaintiffs' Summons, Complaint, Amendment to Complaint and Statement of Damages were personally served on defendant Lorentzen Co's Authorized Agent for Service of Process on January 19, 2016.
Default as to defendant Lorentzen Co was entered on March 11, 2016.
Statment of Damages was filed on March 11, 2016.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on October 21, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Harold Montano and Janet Montano, and against the Defaulted Defendant Lorentzen Co., as follows:

Economic Damages as to plaintiff Harold Montano - $659,338.24.
Non-Economic Damages as to plaintiff Harold Montano - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Janet Montano - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $20,659,338.24.

Proposed Judgment as to all three defendants was submitted to and signed by the Court.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

On Dec. 13, 2016 plaintiffs notified the Court of their submission to the Court's tentatives therefore tentative ruling was adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial, advancing the current trial date; and extending discovery cutoff is denied without prejudice. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate that Mr. Montano's health is such that preference is necessary to prevent prejudicing his interest in the litigation, as required by C.C.P. 36(a). Order submitted by defendant ControlCo was signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported (503/GLW)
